spec_match.rationale: The live support probe now uses a real quirks-mode iframe fragment-navigation test for differently cased <a name> anchors, but the remaining visual explorer still uses CSS Anchor Positioning terminology that does not match this HTML fragment feature.

interactive.rationale: Allows typing custom anchor names and positioned tooltip anchor references and immediately calculates whether they resolve or mismatch under Standard, Quirks (pre-150), and Quirks (150+) rules, with live visual connected/disconnected simulation block.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The repaired probe matches the parent HTML fragment-navigation feature, while the visible matrix still needs terminology cleanup from anchor-name/position-anchor to <a name>/URL fragment.

edge_cases.rationale: The support probe now checks the actual quirks fragment mismatch behavior; the interactive visual box remains simulated rather than navigating real iframes for arbitrary user-entered pairs.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus link was corrected to feature 5085237991309312 for case-sensitive anchor name matching in quirks mode.

design_system.rationale: Beautiful monospace code layout, active toggle states, and contrast-safe emerald/rose outcome states.

openQuestions.title: Replace CSS Anchor Positioning terminology with HTML fragment terminology
openQuestions.detail: The page still labels fields as anchor-name and position-anchor and shows CSS Anchor Positioning code. Rewrite the visible UI around <a name>, id, and href/location.hash fragment matching so the concept no longer teaches the wrong API surface.

openQuestions.title: Re-review repaired live probe in chrome-devtools-mcp
openQuestions.detail: The live probe or copyable feature-detection snippet was repaired on 2026-06-04. Re-run this route in chrome-devtools-mcp, exercise the visible controls, and verify the repaired probe output plus console/network state before marking the critique fully current.

summary: 2026-06-04 maintenance replaced the inaccurate CSS Anchor Positioning support probe with a real quirks-mode iframe fragment-navigation check and corrected the ChromeStatus id. The remaining visible UI still needs a follow-up rewrite from CSS anchor terminology to HTML <a name>/fragment terminology. chrome-devtools-mcp was unavailable in this session, so browser click-through re-review is still required.
